Tests the `String.prototype.indexOf` method to ensure it returns -1 when the search starts at index 1.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the `indexOf` method on a JavaScript `String` object. It verifies that calling `indexOf` with the substring `"ab"` starting from index `1` in the string `"abcd"` returns `-1`, indicating that the substring is not found starting from that position. This test ensures that the `indexOf` method correctly identifies the absence of the specified substring when the search begins at a given index. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of string manipulation in JavaScript.
